condition.Description: Type of tactile Braille task performed by the participant. Both conditions involve tactile reading of Braille; they differ in the decision rule applied.

Levels.LEXICAL_DECISION: Participant decides whether the Braille character string presented on the line is a real word or a pseudoword.
Levels.6_DOT_DETECTION: Participant decides whether the presented Braille character string contains at least one full six-dot Braille cell.

trial_type.Description: Category of the trial presented to the participant.

Levels.CUE: Instruction screen informing the participant about the upcoming task.
Levels.FIXCROSS: Fixation cross display on the screen; no stimuli were presented and the participant remained passive.
Levels.PSEUDOWORD: A Braille character string corresponding to a pseudoword was presented.
Levels.WORD: A Braille character string corresponding to a real word was presented.
Levels.NO_6-DOT: The presented Braille character string did not contain a full six-dot cell.
Levels.6-DOT: The presented Braille character string contained at least one full six-dot cell.
Levels.RESPONSE: Event marking the participant’s response indicating their lexical decision or detection of a full six-dot cell.

braille_stimulus.Description: Sequence of Braille characters presented to the participant, encoded in six-dot Braille notation. The symbol '#' denotes a Braille cell with all six dots raised (full cell).

response_val.Description: Type of response given by the participant in the current task condition.

Levels.YES: In the LEXICAL_DECISION task, indicates that the participant judged the character string as a real word; in the 6_DOT_DETECTION task, indicates that the participant judged that the string contained at least one full six-dot cell.
Levels.NO: In the LEXICAL_DECISION task, indicates that the participant judged the character string as a pseudoword; in the 6_DOT_DETECTION task, indicates that the participant judged that the string did not contain a full six-dot cell.

response_type.Description: Outcome of the participant’s response in relation to the presented stimulus.

Levels.HIT: Correct detection of a target: in LEXICAL_DECISION, a real word was correctly identified as a word; in 6_DOT_DETECTION, a string containing a full six-dot cell was correctly judged as containing one.
Levels.CORRECT_REJECTION: Participant correctly indicated the absence of a target: in LEXICAL_DECISION, a pseudoword was correctly identified as not a word; in 6_DOT_DETECTION, a string without a full six-dot cell was correctly judged as not containing one.
Levels.FALSE_ALARM: Participant indicated the presence of a target when none was present: in LEXICAL_DECISION, a pseudoword was incorrectly classified as a word; in 6_DOT_DETECTION, a string without a full six-dot cell was incorrectly judged as containing one.
Levels.MISS: Participant failed to respond correctly when a target was present (e.g., a real word or a string containing a full six-dot cell).
